channel.title: dotnet/orleans Pull Requests
channel.description: Pull Requests from https://github.com/dotnet/orleans, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: PR: Fix summary reference on GrainCallFilterExtensions - @Arithmomaniac
item.description: an AddIncomingGrainCallFilter overload mistakenly had IOutgoingGrainCallFilter in its summary.

item.title: PR: Added AWS profile name support to DynamoDBStorage. - @PiotrJustyna
item.description:
This is third and last PR of a series of changes resulting from an issue I reported in May:

https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/issues/7077

Previous PRs:

  1. groundwork refactoring: https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/pull/7083
  2. add aws credentials token: https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/pull/7104

In this PR I'm taking the dynamodb client options one step further allowing for non-default aws profile names (like e.g. sts, which I've been using myself for a while). This change should enrich the spectrum of dynamodb connectivity options further for more people.

Depending on the feedback this PR receives, a follow-up documentation change will have to be made:

https://dotnet.github.io/orleans/docs/grains/grain_persistence/dynamodb_storage.html

which I'm happy to do.

item.title: PR: Add Silo RoleName based placement to 3.5.0 - @Mazurkevichkv
PR: PlacementService: improve performance, reduce directory requests, improve consistency of decisions - @ReubenBond
item.description:
This PR attempts to improve how PlacementService addresses messages in the following ways:

  • Reduce the number of outgoing calls to the grain directory when multiple concurrent requests arrive for the same grain.
  • Improve the consistency of decisions made by ensuring that all concurrent requests to a given grain are given the same address

The basic architecture is that PlacementService has a collection of associated PlacementWorker instances (arbitrarily selected to be one per core) which are responsible for asynchronous address message requests. Messages are consistently assigned to the same PlacementWorker by hashing the TargetGrain id. Each PlacementWorker batches messages for the same grain together so that decisions will be consistent and directory requests will be deduplicated.

item.title: PR: Refactor activation message flow - @ReubenBond
item.description:
This PR changes the way that activations process messages. The core idea is that each activation has an async loop (see ActivationData.RunMessageLoop) which scans pending/running requests and operations (activate/deactivate). Each activation becomes responsible for its own activation/deactivation and for message handling and scheduling. Hopefully, you will agree that the result is a messaging flow which is easier to follow and reason about.

An activation must always be able to handle a message passed to it: whether that means processing it, rejecting it, or forwarding it. This requirement helps to simplify error handling, since it's all the responsibility of the activation now.

Supersedes #7127

item.title: PR: Improve code generator editor experience - @ReubenBond
item.description: There's no first-class way to prevent a Source Generator from running within non-build processes yet, so we will filter those out for now.

item.title: PR: Avoid excessive locking on grainBucketUpdateLock in ClientMessageCenter - @ReubenBond
item.description:
The crucial line is this one:

- && gatewayManager.IsGatewayAvailable(msg.TargetSilo))
+ && gatewayManager.IsGatewayAvailable(existingConnection.RemoteSiloAddress))

Essentially, TargetSilo was almost always un-set (since clients don't know where each grain is active), and so it was always going through ConnectionManager and subsequently UpdateBucket (taking a lock)

item.title: PR: Fix SimpleQueueCache log calls - one of them had a broken log string - @ReubenBond
PR: Clean up Counters logging - @ReubenBond
item.description: Trivial PR Contributes to #7099

item.title: PR: Updated AzureBlobStorage to use state type during JSON deserialization - @benthepoet
item.description:
Currently the AzureBlobStorage persistence provider doesn't use the state type when deserializing JSON. As a result, this makes it so that the provider requires TypeNameHandling.All in order to work.

The other providers all use the state type during JSON deserialization, which allows you to use TypeNameHandling.None.

item.title: PR: Add selector for the client - @andyatwork
item.description: Adds the ability to use a certificate selector for client connections. See issue #7143 .

item.title: PR: Fix streaming caching tests - @benjaminpetit
PR: Add MembershipVersion in GrainAddress - @benjaminpetit
item.description:
Partial port of #7118 for 3.5.0.

Should fix #7054

item.title: PR: Move OnexBoxDeployment to another location - @veikkoeeva
item.description: Moves OnexBoxDeployment to a location that will be determined later. Then also an entry to README.md will be added.

item.title: PR: Add ActivationWorkingSet to monitor recently active activations - @ReubenBond
item.description:
This implements a mark-and-sweep style algorithm for maintaining a set of recently active activations. The benefits of this approach are:

  • Activations do not need to call into ActivationCollector on every request to rechedule their deactivation
  • ActivationData is less coupled to ActivationCollector
  • Both ActivationCollector and IncomingRequestMonitor can subscribe to the lifecycle events of the ActivationWorkingSet type and hence need to perform less bookkeeping themselves.

ActivationWorkingSet implements IActivationWorkingSet:

public interface IActivationWorkingSet
{
    void Add(IActivationWorkingSetMember member);
    void AddOrUnmark(IActivationWorkingSetMember member);
    void Remove(IActivationWorkingSetMember member);
}

IActivationWorkingSetMember has one method: bool IActivationWorkingSetMember.IsCandidateForRemoval(bool wouldRemove). The implementation returns true if the grain is inactive, false otherwise, but also keeps it state up-to-date: if it's inactive and wouldRemove is true, then it knows it will be removed from the working set, so it tracks that in order to add itself when it becomes active again.

IActivationWorkingSetObserver shows the states which a grain passes through:

public interface IActivationWorkingSetObserver
{
    void OnAdded(IActivationWorkingSetMember member);
    void OnActive(IActivationWorkingSetMember member);
    void OnIdle(IActivationWorkingSetMember member);
    void OnRemoved(IActivationWorkingSetMember member);
}

The call flow between the grain and ActivationWorkingSet is like this:

  • Grain created -> ActivationWorkingSet.Add(this)
    • Fire observer.OnAdded(grain) for each observer
  • ActivationWorkingSet examines "active" grain by calling grain.IsCandidateForRemoval(false)
    • True: transition grain to 'idle' and fire observer.OnIdle(grain) for each observer
    • False: keep grain in 'active' state and do nothing
  • ActivationWorkingSet examines "idle" grain by calling grain.IsCandidateForRemoval(true)
    • True: remove grain from ActivationWorkingSet and fire observer.OnRemoved(grain) for each observer
    • False: transition grain to "active" and fire observer.OnActive(grain) for each observer
  • Grain becomes active after returning true for IsCandidateForRemoval(true)
    • Grain calls ActivationWorkingSet.AddOrUnmark(this)
    • Fire observer.OnAdded(grain) for each observer

Of the two observers in this PR (ActivationCollector and IncomingRequestMonitor), each is interested in different events.

ActivationCollector needs to ensure that all grains are eventually collected, but only needs to reschedule collection when a grain becomes active again after being inactive. The collector checks if a grain is eligible for removal when scanning buckets of activations for removal, too, so consistently active grains will not be collected:

  • OnAdded => ScheduleCollection(activation)
  • OnActive => TryRescheduleCollection(activation)
  • OnIdle => no-op
  • OnRemoved => if (activation.CollectionTicket == default) TryRescheduleCollection(activation)

IncomingRequestMonitor is only interested in recently active grains:

  • OnAdded => OnActive
  • OnActive => MarkRecentlyUsed(activation)
  • OnIdle => TryRemove(activation)
  • OnRemoved => OnIdle

item.title: PR: EditorConfig - prefix private fields with '_' - @ReubenBond
item.description: No need to go and update every file at once just yet, but this style is terser and it's what the rest of ASP.NET uses, so we're conforming

item.title: PR: Added Datadog implementation of IMetricTelemetryConsumer. - @PiotrJustyna
item.description:
This change enables Datadog APM support.

At the moment the consumer is very basic, but:

  • it does the job of sending all metrics to datadog
  • serves as a foundation for future changes:
    • telemetry consumer configuration (as defined in e.g. NRTelemetryConsumerConfigurationExtensions)
    • other telemetry consumers:
      • IEventTelemetryConsumer
      • IExceptionTelemetryConsumer
      • IDependencyTelemetryConsumer
      • IRequestTelemetryConsumer

I'm not sure this should be packable, so looking forward to some feedback, but technically this is working (albeit quite basic) code.

item.title: PR: Remove logging noise from MemoryStorageGrain - @ReubenBond
item.description: Currently, it logs when it activates or deactivates Part of #7099

item.title: PR: Set a default ClusterId and ServiceId - @ReubenBond
item.description: Papercut (#7099)

item.title: PR: [3.5.0] Upgrade SDK & fix projects - @ReubenBond
item.description:
In order to be able to backport support for C# 9.0 records, we need to first upgrade the .NET SDK which we use to build Orleans itself (since we need to later upgrade the version of Roslyn used by the code generator).

This should not affect users - I tested that locally using SDK 3.1.410 and targeting netcoreapp3.1

Fixes #6898

item.title: PR: [Backport] Add C# 9.0 `record` support to serializer (#7108) - @ReubenBond
item.description: Backport for 3.5.0

item.title: PR: In GrainId, use concrete types instead of string and store the MembershipVersion - @benjaminpetit
item.description: The field MembershipVersion in the GrainId struct will help with address invalidation

item.title: PR: Remove SiloHostBuilder - @ReubenBond
item.description:
Orleans is all-in on the .NET Generic Host, aka HostBuilder. For quite some time, we have been shipping IHostBuilder.UseOrleans(...) extension methods to configure an ISiloBuilder instance and have had no need for the ISiloHostBuilder interface or the SiloHostBuilder class, which are effectively the union of ISiloBuilder and IHostBuilder.

In an attempt to remove future confusion between the builder types, this PR removes ISiloHostBuilder, SiloHostBuilder and any references to those types. This also involved removing the Microsoft.Orleans.Hosting.ServiceFabric package, which is obsolete in the presence of the generic host builder.

Contributes to #7099

item.title: PR: Fix how README files are linked within samples - @ReubenBond
PR: Overhaul samples - @ReubenBond
item.description:
This PR overhauls the samples in the following ways:

  • All samples updated to Orleans 3.4.x
  • Sample folder structure flattened - no more 1.x, 2.0, 2.1, 2.3, 3.0, 3.1, 3.3 folders
  • All samples have a README.md, usually with images (screenshots, diagrams, etc)
  • Top-level README.md which indexes all of the samples and describes what each one demonstrates
  • Samples use .NET Generic Host with .UseOrleans instead of SiloHostBuilder
  • Many samples simplified
  • Added a Kubernetes sample (named Voting)

Several samples were removed:

  • AdHocReactiveCaching - ideally, we could move this to a separate repository and link it from the top-level README in an Advanced Samples
  • HelloWorldCpp - this C++ sample involves manually hosting CoreCLR and is too advanced for an in-repo sample. It could also be an advanced sample in an external repository

I believe we should also migrate the OneBoxDeployment sample into an external repository with a link from the top-level README. cc @veikkoeeva. I have left it where it is for now.

item.title: PR: Add c#9 record support to serializer - @oising
item.description:
Enables support for serialization of c# 9 records for grain method return values and parameters.

Thanks to @ReubenBond for the guiding hand.

item.title: PR: Added token to DynamoDB client configuration. - @PiotrJustyna
item.description:
As a follow up to https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/pull/7083, I'm introducing tokens in dynamodb client configuration. This resolves problems where authentication relies not only on:

  • access key
  • secret key

but also on tokens. At the moment:

  • orleans documentation does not describe such situations: https://dotnet.github.io/orleans/docs/grains/grain_persistence/dynamodb_storage.html
  • token-based dynamodb authentication is not supported

This change allows for both types of authentication credentials:

  • access key & secret key
  • access key & secret key & token

Usage:

siloBuilder.AddDynamoDBGrainStorage(
  name: "profileStore",
  configureOptions: options =>
  {
      options.UseJson = true;
      options.AccessKey = "***";
      options.SecretKey = "***";
      options.Token = "***";
      options.Service = "***";
  });

options.Token can simply be omitted if not needed/not present.

More details in my draft PR https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/pull/7082

item.title: PR: In PooledQueueCache, avoid cache miss if we know that we didn't missed any event - @benjaminpetit
item.description: Port to main of #7060

item.title: PR: Create missing OrleansQuery table in pg sql script. - @ushenkodmitry
item.description: Creation of missing table OrleansQuery was added to the pg sql script.

item.title: PR: Consolidated DynamoDBClientOptions - @PiotrJustyna
item.description:
In preparation for https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/pull/7082, as a result of feedback I received https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/pull/7082#pullrequestreview-659888375 and inspired by the PR referenced in the feedback: https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/pull/7019 I am first consolidating all DynamoDBClientOptions in order not to repeat reused properties.

In order to complete https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/pull/7082 my goal is to eventually extend DynamoDBClientOptions with two more properties:

  • string Token (holding one's AWS authentication token) - this is to support DynamoDB connections requiring not only access key and secret key, but also a token. This is not supported today.
  • string ProfileName (name of one's named profile used to authenticate with AWS) - this is to support non-default named profiles while authenticating DynamoDB clients.

item.title: PR: AddDynamoDBGrainStorage - support for tokens and non-default profile names. - @PiotrJustyna
item.description:
In response to https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/issues/7077 I'm introducing two changes:

  • fix which corrects situations where callers have a token accompanying:

    • AccessKey
    • SecretKey

    For more details, see: https://dotnet.github.io/orleans/docs/grains/grain_persistence/dynamodb_storage.html.

    In those cases, providing only AccessKey and SecretKey (and not the token) results in an exception. Please refer to the linked issue for more details.

    Before:

    siloBuilder.AddDynamoDBGrainStorage(
  name: "profileStore",
  configureOptions: options =>
  {
      options.UseJson = true;
      options.AccessKey = "***";
      options.SecretKey = "***";
      options.Service = "***";
  });

    After:

    siloBuilder.AddDynamoDBGrainStorage(
  name: "profileStore",
  configureOptions: options =>
  {
      options.UseJson = true;
      options.AccessKey = "***";
      options.SecretKey = "***";
      options.Token = "***";
      options.Service = "***";
  });

    The fix still supports scenarios where the token is not required. Then, it can simply be omitted.

  • Support for non-default profile names stored in ~/.aws/credentials. When AWS credentials are not provided either through environment variables or explicitly via options, AWS SDK falls back on credentials stored under the default ~/.aws/credentials profile. In case people chose not to have the default profile in that file or use multiple profiles, using Orleans DynamoDB connectivity could be tricky. My change adds a profile name from which the credentials can be retrieved. Sample credentials file storing multiple profiles can look like this:

    [default]
aws_access_key_id = ***
aws_secret_access_key = ***
aws_security_token = ***
aws_session_expiration = ***
aws_session_token = ***

[sts]
aws_access_key_id = ***
aws_secret_access_key = ***
aws_security_token = ***
aws_session_expiration = ***
aws_session_token = ***

item.title: PR: Fix SonarQube's "Any() should be used to test for emptiness" / Code S… - @marodev
item.description:
Hello,

This pull request is very simple and aims to reduce the technical debt. It specifically targets the "'Any()' should be used to test for emptiness" rule in SonarQube.

Let me know if you are interested in similar contributions.

Best, Martin

item.title: PR: Fix SQL Server connection string - @veikkoeeva
item.description: The newer SQL Server ADO.NET drivers do not accept "asynchronous processing" as a parameter as it's on by default and cannot be changed.

item.title: PR: Add Orleans.Serialization library as a high-fidelity, version-tolerant serializer - @ReubenBond
item.description:
This PR integrates a new version-tolerant serializer and RPC primitives into Orleans. For more context, and prior discussion, please see #2653 and https://github.com/ReubenBond/Hagar.

The serializer implemented in this PR requires that developers annotate their types with an attribute on the type and an attribute on each serialized field/property. There is an included code analyzer to ease the process, generating the per-data-member attributes. Here is an example of an annotated type:

[GenerateSerializer]
public class ClassWithEnumTestData
{
    [Id(0)]
    public TestEnum EnumValue { get; set; }

    [Id(1)]
    public CampaignEnemyTestType Enemy { get; set; }
}

The wire format takes inspiration from Bond, and Protocol Buffers, but adds support for nominal types and references, which would otherwise need to be encoded on top of the base protocol. This is the primary reason for its existence, since it is believed that this greatly improves the experience for .NET developers, giving them substantially more freedom when designing their applications and modelling their data.

Developers can configure the system to use Newtonsoft.Json or another package as the serializer for their types instead, similarly to how IExternalSerializer works today. Unlike the existing serializer, even data serialized by external serializers must conform to a basic wire format (typically, by emitting a length-prefixed sequence).

Deep copiers are decoupled from serializers, which is an issue with the existing system: developers often do not want to replace copiers when replacing serializers.

Previously unserializable/undeserializable Exceptions can be serialized with a decent degree of fidelity using this serializer, so that the receiver can see the exception type, message, stack trace, and other core properties even if the concrete exception type is not available. If the concrete exception type is not available, then the properties are deserialized into a special-purpose UnavailableExceptionFallbackException object.

A new NuGet package, named Orleans.Sdk, takes the place of installing multiple NuGet packages on grain interface/flass/

Application Parts are removed entirely: the code generator package generates attributes which serve the purpose of Application Parts in identifying the closure of assemblies which contain types that Orleans must know about. There is still a way to remove grain classes & interfaces programmatically, by configuring GrainTypeOptions, which has a Classes and an Interfaces property. Assemblies can also be manually included in the closure by adding an assembly-level attribute to the main assembly.

While Orleans serialization is not intended for use between untrusted parties, this serializer greatly improves security aspects of serialization in a variety of ways:

  • Only known types are allowed to be serialized - types are disabled by default. Types which are annotated are allowed to be deserialized, and predicates for which types are allowed can be specified at configuration time.
  • Length fields are validated to prevent an attacker to force the deserializing party to pre-allocate a buffer which is larger than the payload.

The serializer is also much faster than the existing serializer, making use of hardware intrinsics where available, and avoiding unnecessary allocations.

RPC

Serialization and RPC are closely related. A large component of RPC is the serialization of method calls for later deserialization and invocation. This PR also includes new RPC primitives, which replace both InvokeMethodRequest and generated IGrainMethodInvoker implementations with generated IInvokable classes (two become one). There is one generated IInvokable class for each method on each grain interface. Generated GrainReference classes change minorly to make use of the new generated IInvokable classes.

The RPC supports efficient serialization of method arguments, avoiding most or all unnecessary allocations and avoiding unnecessary type information

As a part of a concerted effort to reduce unnecessary overheads, the RPC is customizable enough to allow us to implement Orleans.Transactions entirely outside of the core libraries, and this PR also includes that change. This means that non-transactional calls avoid checking for transaction contexts or requirements. It hopefully also means that we can support more experimentation in the future without requiring forking the codebase.

TODOs for this PR:

  • [x] Implement renaming tolerance for RPC invokable types

Areas for future investigation/improvements:

  • [ ] Move existing SerializationManager and related types to a new package (Microsoft.Orleans.Serialization.Legacy)
  • [ ] Analyzer: allow customization of the serializer attributes through build time properties, just as the code generator does
  • [ ] Add in-place copying optimization for RPC types, supporting [Immutable] on parameters and return values (via [return: Immutable])
  • [ ] Explore emitting a separate, diffable database of types included in an application, so that annotations can be removed from code. This may end up being too 'magic' to be worthwhile, but we won't know for sure unless we explore it.

Fixes #2653 Fixes #5021

item.title: PR: Do not retry handshake with consumer if agent is shutting down - @benjaminpetit
PR: [Logging] Use Microsoft.Logging.Extensions directly - @waitxd
item.description:
Part of #7025.

This PR replaces usage of Orleans legacy logging wrappers with Microsoft.Logging.Extensions.

To ease review this PR doesn't try to change logging templates, introduce named arguments nor make templates compile time constant. It will be made in following PRs.

Internal extension classes are removed. Public one is marked with [Obsolete] (BC involved).

item.title: PR: Add Silo RoleName based placement - @Mazurkevichkv
item.description:
Added new directory for role name based placement strategy

[SiloRoleBasedPlacement] would be useful for scenarios with the requirements of having single grain per silo type. Example of usage this.membershipTableManager.MembershipTableSnapshot.Entries.TryGetValue(this.localSiloDetails.SiloAddress, out MembershipEntry entry); await this.client.GetGrain<IGrainWithRoleBasedPlacement>(entry.RoleName).Ping();

item.title: PR: In PooledQueueCache, avoid cache miss if we know that we didn't missed any event - @benjaminpetit
item.description:
Current issue

In the current implementation of PooledQueueCache (the cache used notably by the EventHub streaming provider), in order to advance a cursor from event N to event N+1, both events need to be in cache, to ensure that nothing was missed between the two.

That means that if event N was evicted, trying to move the cursor will throw a QueueCacheMissException, even if the subscriber already consumed this event and is waiting for event N+1. This behavior can be really confusing for streaming users.

Proposed change

In this PR, the PooledQueueCache keeps a dictionary of StreamId -> StreamSequenceToken to keep track of the token from the last evicted event for a given stream.

If the consumer already processed event N and this event is not in cache:

  • If event N was the last evicted event for this stream, we didn't missed anything. We restart the cursor from the oldest message in the cache
  • If event N is older than the last evicted event from this stream: we know for sure we missed some events, so we will throw an exception

It is possible to opt-out by setting StreamCacheEvictionOptions.MetadataMinTimeInCache to null
